title: Unfortunately Yours
author: Tessa Bailey
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Unfortunately Yours" book by Tessa Bailey?
Options:
A. However, August persists in attempting and failing and attempting again, and ultimately he will succeed. I am confident he will. And when he does, it will be incredible, because he is doing it manually. Through the sweat of his brow. It will signify something more profound than money. She had become so engrossed in her words that she hadn't noticed Ingram had set his glass down on the table and was looking at her solemnly. Without the customary smirk for once. We should all be fortunate enough to have someone place such faith in us as you do in your husband, Ms. Vos. Mrs. Cates, she amended with an embarrassed grin.
B. But August keeps trying and failing and trying again, and eventually he's going to get it right. I know he will. And when he does, it's going to be amazing, because he's doing it with his own two hands. Through hard work. It will mean more to him than money. She had gotten so caught up in what she was saying that she didn't realize Ingram had put his glass down on the table and was looking at her seriously. Without his usual smirk for once. We should all be so lucky to have someone believe in us the way you believe in your husband, Ms. Vos. Mrs. Cates, she corrected with an awkward smile.  
C. However, August continues attempting and being unsuccessful and trying again, and finally he is going to succeed. I am certain he will. And when he does, it will be incredible, because he is doing it manually. Through his own effort. It will be more meaningful than money to him. She had become so immersed in her words that she was unaware Ingram had lowered his glass to the table and was regarding her solemnly. Without the characteristic smirk for once. We should all be fortunate enough to have someone place such confidence in us as you do in your husband, Ms. Vos. Mrs. Cates, she amended with an uncomfortable smile.
D. But August continues to try and fail and try againand eventually hes going to get it. I know he is. And when he does, its going to be amazing, because hes doing it by hand. By the sweat of his brow. Its going to mean something more than money. Shed gotten so lost in her speech, she didnt realize Ingram had lowered his glass to the table and was regarding her seriously. Minus the smirk for once. We should all be so lucky to have someone believe in us the way you believe in your husband, Ms. Vos. Mrs. Cates, she corrected with a flustered smile.
Answer:
